title: ROUGE vs. BLEU Tradeoffs
concept: BLEU is precision-focused (translation safety); ROUGE is recall-focused (summarization coverage).

exercises.prompt: What is the core difference in focus between the BLEU and ROUGE metrics?

options.text: BLEU is precision-focused; ROUGE is recall-focused.
BLEU is recall-focused; ROUGE is precision-focused.
Both metrics only look at the speed of the model.

exercises.explain: BLEU focuses on precision to ensure translations are safe and accurate, while ROUGE focuses on recall to ensure summaries cover all necessary details.

cards.title: Which is Better?
cards.body:
Imagine writing an essay. Would you rather get a perfect grade for spelling every single word correctly, or for including all the facts your teacher asked for? 

AI text evaluators have to make this exact choice! BLEU and ROUGE look at AI text through two completely different lenses.

cards.title: Precision vs. Recall
cards.body:
BLEU and ROUGE are evaluation tools with different priorities. 

BLEU prioritizes precision: it checks if the AI's words actually belong in the correct answer. 

ROUGE prioritizes recall: it checks if the AI managed to capture all the important ideas from the correct answer.

cards.title: The Packing Dilemma
cards.body:
Imagine you are packing a suitcase for a trip. 

If you use BLEU, you are a super careful packer: you only pack things you are 100% sure you need (Precision). No extra junk allowed! 

If you use ROUGE, you are an over-packer: you want to make sure you don't leave anything important behind (Recall). You want to cover all your bases!

cards.title: Summarizing the Chase
cards.body:
Let's look at a short story summary:
Original: "The cat chased the mouse under the red sofa."

If the AI writes: "The cat chased the mouse."
• BLEU gives a high score because every word it wrote is 100% correct (high precision).
• ROUGE gives a lower score because it missed the important detail about the "red sofa" (low recall).

cards.title: Myth: One Score Rules Them All
cards.body:
People often think one score is simply "better" than the other. 

In reality, they just have different jobs! Using only BLEU for summaries might reward an AI for writing a single perfect sentence while ignoring the rest of the book. You need the right tool for the right job.

cards.title: Metric Face-Off
cards.body: Let's compare the goals of BLEU and ROUGE side-by-side to see how they look at the same AI output differently.

left.text: Wants to avoid mistakes. Prefers short, perfectly accurate translations.

right.text: Wants to capture everything. Prefers summaries that cover all main points.

exercises.prompt: If you are building a language translation app and want to make sure the AI doesn't hallucinate or add random incorrect words, which metric should you prioritize?

options.text: Neither of them care about accuracy.
Both only count the total number of characters.

exercises.explain: BLEU is precision-focused. It penalizes translation errors heavily by checking if the generated words match the reference words exactly.

exercises.prompt: True or False: BLEU is precision-focused, while ROUGE is recall-focused.
exercises.explain: BLEU is precision-focused to ensure translation safety, whereas ROUGE is recall-focused to ensure all main points are captured in a summary.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the description of ROUGE's main goal.
exercises.sentence: Because ROUGE measures how much of the original text was captured, it is focused on recall.
exercises.explain: ROUGE focuses on recall because summaries need to cover all the main ideas from the source text.

exercises.prompt: Which of these outputs would get a higher ROUGE score for a summary of a history chapter?

options.text: A long summary containing all key facts but with some extra words.
A tiny three-word sentence that is 100% correct but misses most facts.
A completely random sentence about space travel.

exercises.explain: The first summary captures all the details (good recall/ROUGE), while the second is short but contains zero mistakes (good precision/BLEU). The third is completely wrong.

exercises.prompt: You are building a new AI. Why would you choose BLEU for a translation chatbot, but ROUGE for a book summarizer?

options.text: BLEU wants to capture every single detail, while ROUGE only cares about short words.
BLEU focuses on precision to avoid bad words, while ROUGE focuses on recall to capture all key points.
BLEU and ROUGE do the exact same thing but use different programming languages.
BLEU is only for writing poetry, while ROUGE is only used to calculate math equations.

exercises.explain: BLEU is precision-focused (did we generate only correct words to avoid translation mistakes?) while ROUGE is recall-focused (did we capture all the key ideas of the summary?).
